domain: IT Service Management — Incident Root Cause Analysis
source_files: incident_event_log.csv (Primary file — enriched event log with 141,712 events across 24,918 incidents)
NOTE: Single-file dataset. Ontology compensates by decomposing the file into 7 derived logical tables via feature engineering (see decomposition_strategy below).
description: Ontology for automated Root Cause Analysis (RCA) hypothesis generation over the ITSM Incident Management dataset. Although the source is a single CSV, it contains rich embedded multi-entity structure covering Incidents, Activities/Events, Assignments (Team + Engineer), Services/Categories, Time Windows, and Resolution outcomes. The file is decomposed into 7 logical node types via pandas groupby operations. The root_cause_group column provides explicit ground truth for RCA verification — this is the most direct ground truth of the three datasets. Multi-hop paths traverse: Incident → Activity → Team → Engineer → ServiceCategory → Priority → TimeWindow.

decomposition_strategy.description: How the single CSV is decomposed into logical relational tables for ontology instantiation

derived_tables.derivation: GROUP BY number (incident ID) — one row per unique incident, aggregating all event-level attributes
Each row in the raw event log is one activity/event within an incident lifecycle (opened, in progress, resolved, closed)
GROUP BY assignment_group — one row per unique support team, with derived performance metrics
GROUP BY assigned_to — one row per unique engineer, with derived workload and resolution metrics
GROUP BY category + subcategory — one row per unique category-subcategory pair
Derived from opened_at timestamp — year, month, quarter, day of week, hour, shift
GROUP BY root_cause_group + closed_code — one row per unique resolution pattern

datasource.description: Core incident records — state, priority, timestamps, RCA labels

Incident.IncidentID: Text | Unique incident number (field: number). Primary key. Format: INCxxxxxxx.
Incident.OpenedAt: DateTime | Timestamp when the incident was first opened/reported.
Incident.ResolvedAt: DateTime | Timestamp when the incident was resolved. NULL if still open.
Incident.ClosedAt: DateTime | Timestamp when the incident was closed after resolution confirmation.
Incident.State: Text | Current state of the incident — New, In Progress, On Hold, Resolved, Closed, Canceled.
Incident.Priority: Text | Incident priority — 1-Critical, 2-High, 3-Moderate, 4-Low. Drives SLA response targets.
Incident.Category: Text | High-level service category affected (e.g., Hardware, Software, Network, Database).
Incident.Subcategory: Text | Subcategory within the category (e.g., Server Hardware, Operating System, Email).
Incident.SymptomDescription: Text | Free-text description of the incident symptoms as reported.
Incident.ResolutionNotes: Text | Free-text description of how the incident was resolved.
Incident.RootCauseGroup: Text | Explicit root cause classification (ground truth for RCA verification). Examples: Software Bug, Hardware Failure, Configuration Error, User Error, Network Issue, Capacity Problem.
Incident.ClosedCode: Text | Resolution outcome code — Solved (Permanently), Solved (Work Around), Not Solved (Not Reproducible), Closed/Resolved by Caller.
Incident.ResolutionTimeHours: Numeric | Derived — hours between OpenedAt and ResolvedAt. Primary KPI for incident management efficiency.
Incident.ResponseTimeHours: Numeric | Derived — hours between OpenedAt and first assignment (first In Progress state).
Incident.TotalReopenCount: Numeric | Derived — count of times the incident moved back from Resolved to In Progress. High reopen counts indicate poor initial diagnosis.
Incident.IsBreachedSLA: Boolean | Derived — 1 if ResolutionTimeHours exceeds SLA target for this Priority level. SLA targets: P1=4h, P2=8h, P3=72h, P4=168h.
Incident.SLABreachHours: Numeric | Derived — hours by which the SLA was exceeded. Negative = resolved within SLA.
Incident.AssignmentGroupChanges: Numeric | Derived — count of distinct assignment groups this incident passed through. High counts indicate ping-pong routing failures.
Incident.TotalActivities: Numeric | Derived — total number of event log rows for this incident. Proxy for investigation complexity.

datasource.description: Event log rows — one row per state transition per incident

Activity.ActivityID: Text | Derived unique identifier for each event log row (IncidentID + sequence number).
Activity.IncidentID: Text | Foreign key to Incident node.
Activity.State: Text | State of the incident at this activity event.
Activity.AssignmentGroup: Text | Team assigned at this activity step (foreign key to Team node).
Activity.AssignedTo: Text | Engineer assigned at this activity step (foreign key to Engineer node).
Activity.ActivityTimestamp: DateTime | Timestamp of this activity event (sys_updated_on).
Activity.ActivityDurationHours: Numeric | Derived — hours between this activity and the next activity in the same incident. Time spent in this state.
Activity.IsReassignment: Boolean | Derived — 1 if AssignmentGroup differs from the previous activity's AssignmentGroup.
Activity.IsReopening: Boolean | Derived — 1 if State transitions from Resolved back to In Progress.

datasource.description: Support team performance — SLA breach rate, reopen rate, specialisation

Team.TeamName: Text | Human-readable name of the support team.
Team.TotalIncidentsAssigned: Numeric | Derived — total number of incidents ever assigned to this team.
Team.AvgResolutionTimeHours: Numeric | Derived — average resolution time for incidents resolved by this team. Primary team performance KPI.
Team.SLABreachRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of this team's incidents that breached SLA. Range 0-1.
Team.AvgReopenCount: Numeric | Derived — average number of reopens for incidents handled by this team. High values indicate poor diagnosis quality.
Team.PingPongRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of incidents where this team reassigned to another team after first accepting. Indicates routing mismatches.
Team.SpecializationScore: Numeric | Derived — entropy-based measure of how focused the team is on specific categories. Low entropy = specialized team.
Team.TeamPerformanceScore: Numeric | Derived — composite score: (1 - SLABreachRate) * 0.5 + (1 - AvgReopenCount/MAX_REOPEN) * 0.3 + (1 - PingPongRate) * 0.2. Range 0-1.

datasource.description: Engineer performance — resolution time, reopen rate, workload

Engineer.TeamID: Text | Foreign key to Team node — the team this engineer primarily belongs to.
Engineer.TotalIncidentsHandled: Numeric | Derived — total incidents this engineer was assigned to.
Engineer.AvgResolutionTimeHours: Numeric | Derived — average resolution time for incidents resolved by this engineer.
Engineer.SLABreachRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of this engineer's incidents that breached SLA.
Engineer.AvgReopenCount: Numeric | Derived — average reopens for incidents this engineer resolved. High values indicate resolution quality issues.
Engineer.UniqueTeamsWorkedWith: Numeric | Derived — count of distinct teams this engineer has been assigned under. High values indicate cross-team utility.
Engineer.ResolutionAccuracyRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of incidents resolved with ClosedCode = 'Solved (Permanently)' vs workarounds.
Engineer.ActiveIncidentLoad: Numeric | Derived — number of open incidents currently assigned to this engineer (snapshot metric).

datasource.description: Pre-aggregated category-level SLA and RCA statistics

ServiceCategory.CategoryID: Text | Derived composite key — Category + '_' + Subcategory.
ServiceCategory.Category: Text | High-level service category (e.g., Hardware, Software, Network, Database, Email).
ServiceCategory.TotalIncidents: Numeric | Derived — total incidents in this category-subcategory combination.
ServiceCategory.AvgResolutionTimeHours: Numeric | Derived — average resolution time for incidents in this category.
ServiceCategory.SLABreachRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of incidents in this category that breached SLA.
ServiceCategory.MostCommonRootCause: Text | Derived — most frequent RootCauseGroup for incidents in this category. Key RCA insight.
ServiceCategory.RootCauseDiversity: Numeric | Derived — number of distinct root cause groups observed for this category. High diversity = complex diagnostic category.
ServiceCategory.AvgPriority: Numeric | Derived — average numeric priority of incidents in this category (1=highest). Indicates category criticality.
ServiceCategory.RecurrenceRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of incidents in this category that are reopened. High values suggest systemic unresolved issues.

datasource.description: RCA ground truth — root cause labels and resolution patterns

ResolutionOutcome.OutcomeID: Text | Derived composite key — RootCauseGroup + '_' + ClosedCode.
ResolutionOutcome.RootCauseGroup: Text | Root cause classification — the primary ground truth for RCA verification. Values: Software Bug, Hardware Failure, Configuration Error, User Error, Network Issue, Capacity Problem, Third Party Issue, Unknown.
ResolutionOutcome.ClosedCode: Text | Resolution outcome code — Solved (Permanently), Solved (Work Around), Not Solved (Not Reproducible), Closed/Resolved by Caller.
ResolutionOutcome.FrequencyCount: Numeric | Derived — total incidents with this RootCauseGroup + ClosedCode combination.
ResolutionOutcome.AvgResolutionTimeHours: Numeric | Derived — average resolution time for incidents with this outcome pattern.
ResolutionOutcome.MostAffectedCategory: Text | Derived — most common ServiceCategory for incidents with this root cause.
ResolutionOutcome.MostAffectedTeam: Text | Derived — most common Team for incidents with this root cause.
ResolutionOutcome.IsPermanentResolutionRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion resolved permanently vs workaround for this root cause. Low values indicate recurring unresolved causes.
ResolutionOutcome.SLABreachRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of incidents with this root cause that breached SLA.

datasource.description: Pre-aggregated time-window incident volume and SLA stats

TimeWindow.OpenYear: Numeric | Year when the incident was opened.
TimeWindow.OpenMonth: Numeric | Month (1-12) when the incident was opened.
TimeWindow.OpenQuarter: Numeric | Quarter (1-4) when the incident was opened.
TimeWindow.OpenDayOfWeek: Numeric | Day of week (0=Monday, 6=Sunday) of incident opening.
TimeWindow.OpenHour: Numeric | Hour (0-23) of incident opening timestamp.
TimeWindow.Shift: Text | Derived — Business Hours (8-17 weekdays), After Hours (18-23 weekdays), Weekend, Night (0-7).
TimeWindow.IsMonthEnd: Boolean | Derived — 1 if day of month is 25-31. Month-end periods often see higher incident volumes due to batch job failures.
TimeWindow.AvgIncidentsPerDay: Numeric | Derived — average daily incident count in this time period.
TimeWindow.AvgResolutionTimeHours: Numeric | Derived — average resolution time for incidents opened in this period.
TimeWindow.SLABreachRateInPeriod: Numeric | Derived — proportion of incidents opened in this period that breached SLA.
TimeWindow.MostCommonRootCauseInPeriod: Text | Derived — most frequent root cause group for incidents opened in this period.

relationtrees.description: Directed paths through the 7 derived logical node types. Multi-hop traversals enable RCA hypothesis generation about why incidents take long to resolve, which teams struggle, and what root causes recur.
multi_hop_example_paths: Incident → Activity → Team → ServiceCategory (4 hops): Are certain teams being assigned incidents outside their service category specialization — causing longer resolution times?
Incident → ResolutionOutcome → Team → TimeWindow (4 hops): Do certain root causes (e.g., Configuration Error) cluster in specific time windows (e.g., month-end batch runs)?
Incident → Activity → Engineer → Team → SLABreachRate (5 hops): Are specific engineers with high reopen counts concentrated in teams with high SLA breach rates — suggesting team-level training gaps?
ServiceCategory → Incident → TimeWindow → RootCauseGroup (4 hops): Does the 'Network' category show different root cause distributions during after-hours vs business-hours windows?

Incident → AssignmentGroupChanges → Team → SpecializationScore (4 hops): Do incidents with 3+ assignment group changes predominantly involve generalist teams (high category diversity) rather than specialized ones?

axioms.name: SLA breach detection
axioms.rule: IF (Priority='1-Critical' AND ResolutionTimeHours > 4) OR (Priority='2-High' AND ResolutionTimeHours > 8) OR (Priority='3-Moderate' AND ResolutionTimeHours > 72) OR (Priority='4-Low' AND ResolutionTimeHours > 168) THEN Incident.IsBreachedSLA = TRUE

axioms.name: Ping-pong routing failure
axioms.rule: IF Incident.AssignmentGroupChanges >= 3 THEN Incident.IsRoutingFailure = TRUE AND Incident.RootCauseHypothesis INCLUDES 'Incorrect initial routing'

axioms.name: Chronic reopen pattern
axioms.rule: IF Incident.TotalReopenCount >= 2 THEN RootCauseGroup LIKELY IN ['Software Bug', 'Capacity Problem', 'Configuration Error']

axioms.name: After-hours resolution penalty
axioms.rule: IF TimeWindow.Shift IN ['After Hours', 'Night', 'Weekend'] THEN AVG(ResolutionTimeHours) > 1.5 * AVG(ResolutionTimeHours | Shift='Business Hours')

axioms.name: Team-category mismatch
axioms.rule: IF Team.SpecializationScore < 0.3 AND Incident.Category NOT IN Team.TopCategories THEN P(IsBreachedSLA) > 0.6

axioms.name: Month-end batch failure cluster
axioms.rule: IF TimeWindow.IsMonthEnd = TRUE AND ServiceCategory.Category IN ['Database', 'Software'] THEN IncidentVolume > 1.3 * MonthlyAvgVolume

ground_truth_strategy.primary: RootCauseGroup column — explicit root cause classification, the most direct RCA ground truth of all three datasets
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_sla: IsBreachedSLA (derived from Priority + ResolutionTimeHours) — deterministic SLA compliance label
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_statistical: IQR outlier detection on ResolutionTimeHours per Category + Priority — flags abnormally slow resolutions
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_temporal: Rolling weekly z-score on AvgIncidentsPerDay per TimeWindow — flags volume spikes
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_multihop: AssignmentGroupChanges >= 3 — deterministic routing failure flag for ping-pong hypothesis verification
ground_truth_strategy.note: RootCauseGroup enables exact automated verification unlike DS1 (statistical) or DS2 (statistical) — AX1 verification is 100% deterministic

hypothesis_examples: Incidents in the 'Network' category that occur on weekends and holidays take 2.3× longer to resolve than weekday incidents — root cause is reduced after-hours staffing for network specialists, not incident complexity.

The 'Configuration Error' root cause group accounts for 34% of all SLA breaches in the 'Software' category — these incidents are being misrouted to Tier 1 teams that lack configuration management access.

Incidents with 3 or more assignment group changes have a SLA breach rate 4× higher than single-assignment incidents — the root cause is inaccurate initial incident categorization at the point of logging.
Engineers with active incident loads above 15 simultaneously show reopen rates 60% higher than engineers with loads below 5 — indicating capacity-driven resolution quality degradation.
Month-end periods (days 25-31) show a 40% spike in 'Database' category incidents with root cause 'Capacity Problem' — driven by end-of-month batch reporting jobs overwhelming database resources.
